title: Mildly Charred Jamaican Bread Toast
description: A crumbly and nostalgic Jamaican dish featuring cinnamon stick, made with double-rise-bread and cooked in a donabe, this mildly charred toast is a delicious snack, fodmap-friendly and perfect for any time of day.
instructions: Preheat the donabe over medium heat.
Slice the double-rise-bread into 1-inch thick slices.
In a small bowl, mix together the granulated sugar, cinnamon stick pieces, and salt.
Brush the unsalted butter onto each bread slice.
Sprinkle the sugar and cinnamon mixture evenly onto each bread slice.
Dip each bread slice into the beaten eggs, coating both sides.
Place the bread slices into the preheated donabe and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until mildly charred.
Remove the toast from the donabe and place onto a wire rack to cool.
Serve the mildly charred Jamaican bread toast warm and enjoy.
Drizzle with a little vanilla extract, if desired.
